blocks:
Ayahs

لَمْ يَكُنِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ مُنفَكِّينَ حَتَّىٰ تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَةُ (۱)
The faithless from among the People of the Book and the polytheists were not set apart [from the community of the faithful] until the proof had come to them: (1)
رَسُولٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ يَتْلُوا۟ صُحُفًا مُّطَهَّرَةً (۲)
an apostle from Allah reciting impeccable scriptures, (2)
فِيهَا كُتُبٌ قَيِّمَةٌ (۳)
wherein are upright writings. (3)
وَمَا تَفَرَّقَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَةُ (٤)
Those who were given the Book did not divide except after the proof had come to them. (4)

Key Points

Bayyinah (بیّنة) means a clear proof by which truth is distinguished from falsehood. Qayyimah (قیّمة) means straight, firm, valuable, and precious. Qayyim (قیّم) refers to someone who is compassionate and upholds the interests of others. The letter tā’ (ت) in the word Qayyimah is for emphasis (mubālaghah), similar to ‘Allāmah, not for feminization (ta’nīth).

This Ayah has been interpreted in two ways: According to one interpretation, it speaks of the disloyalty and insincerity of the People of the Book and the polytheists. In the other interpretation, it speaks of the completion of the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah) against them.

The claim of the disbelievers was that they would remain on their path until a clear proof reached them. However, even after the clear proof arrived, they remained on their path, and only a few believed. A similar theme is found in Ayah 89 of Sūrat al-Baqarah, where, before the advent of Islam, they were awaiting the arrival of a new Prophet and giving themselves good tidings, but: «فلما جائهم ما عرفوا كفروا» (But when there came to them that which they recognized, they disbelieved).

Ṣuḥuf (صُحُف) is the plural of ṣaḥīfah (صحیفة). It is as if every section of the heavenly book is a ṣaḥīfah on its own. The Torah is one book, yet the Quran says concerning it: «صحف ابراهیم و موسى» (The pages of Abraham and Moses) (96).

96) Al-A‘lā, 19.

Messages

1- Do not expect people to abandon their beliefs and their path without a clear proof. «لم یكن... منفكّین حتى تأتیهم البیّنة»

2- In the completion of the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah), disbelievers and polytheists are not separate from one another. Allah completes the proof for all people, whether disbelievers or polytheists, by sending the Bayyinah. «تأتیهم البیّنة»

3- More is expected of the People of the Book. (Although the first Ayah spoke of the People of the Book and the polytheists, the subsequent Ayahs criticize only the People of the Book.) «و ما تفرّق الّذین اوتوا الكتاب»

4- Allah's completion of the proof is through a heavenly Prophet and a pure Book. «رسول... یتلوا صُحُفًا مطهّرة»

5- The Quran is far removed from every error, deviation, futility, falsehood, and distortion. «صحفاً مطهّرة»

6- The Quran contains stabilizing instructions (97). «فیها كتب قیّمة»

7- The Quran is both a moderate Book, free from crookedness (98), and a cause for the moderation and stability of society (99). «قیّمة»

8- In the presence of carnal desires (hawā an-nafs), knowledge and learning are not problem solvers. The People of the Book clearly understood the truth, but due to their carnal desires, they became subject to division. «و ما تفرّق الّذین اوتوا الكتاب...»

97) The word kutub (كُتب) can mean religious instructions, similar to the Ayah «كتب علیكم الصیام» where kitāb means instruction.

98) If we consider Qayyimah (قیمة) to mean fixed and stable, which in literary terms is an intransitive verb (fi‘l lāzim).

99) If we consider Qayyimah (قیمة) to mean the establisher or upholder, which in Arabic grammar is a transitive verb (muta‘addī) possessing an object (maf‘ūl).

Ayah

وَمَآ أُمِرُوٓا۟ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ حُنَفَآءَ وَيُقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤْتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ ٱلْقَيِّمَةِ (٥)
Though they were not commanded except to worship Allah, dedicating their faith to Him as men of pure faith, and to maintain the prayer and pay the zakat. That is the upright religion. (5)

Key Points

«حنفاء» (Hunafā') is the plural of «حَنیف» (Hanīf), meaning inclination towards the truth, in contrast to the word «جَنیف» (Janīf), which means inclination towards falsehood. If we place the Ayah, "ذلك دین القیّمة" (That is the upright religion), alongside the Ayah, "فاقم وجهك للدّین حنیفاً فطرة اللّه" (So set your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. [It is] the nature [Fiṭrah] of Allah) (100), we can deduce that pure and unadulterated worship, the relationship with God (prayer/Salat), and helping others (charity/Zakat) are among the innate matters (Fiṭrī) of every human being.

100) Rūm, 30.

Messages

1- The tastes and desires of others are changeable. The stable and enduring religion is sincere devotion to the constant God. «مخلصین له الدین... ذلك دین القیّمة»

2- Faith is not firm without prayer (Salat) and charity (Zakat). «یقیموا الصّلاة و یؤتوا الزكاة و ذلك دین القیّمة»

3- Attention to the history of obstinacy and division provides comfort to the Prophet and his followers. «و ما تفرّق الّذین... الاّ من بعد ما جاءتهم البیّنة»

4- Monotheism (Tawhīd) and sincere worship of Allah are factors of unity. «و ما تفرّق الّذین اوتوا الكتاب... و ما امروا الاّ لیعبدوا اللّه»

5- The Prophets invited people toward Allah, not toward themselves. «و ما امروا الاّ لیعبدوا اللّه»

6- Being pure and unadulterated is the secret to permanence. «مخلصین... ذلك دین القیّمة»

7- The command for Monotheism, prayer (Salat), and charity (Zakat) has existed in all Divine religions. «و ما امروا الاّ لیعبدوا اللّه...»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ فِى نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمْ شَرُّ ٱلْبَرِيَّةِ (٦)
Indeed the faithless from among the People of the Book and the polytheists will be in the fire of hell, to remain in it [forever]. It is they who are the worst of creatures. (6)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمْ خَيْرُ ٱلْبَرِيَّةِ (۷)
Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—it is they who are the best of creatures. (7)

Key Points

The word Barīyah (بَریّة) is either derived from barā (بَرى), meaning earth/soil, and refers to all earthly beings, including humans; or it is derived from bara'a (بَرَءَ), meaning creation, and Barīyah is on the measure of fa'īlah (فعیلة) meaning maf'ūlah (مفعولة), i.e., the created beings (creatures).

It has been narrated in traditions (Riwāyāt) from both Shia and Sunni sources that the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said to Ali ibn Abi Talib: "You and your followers are the best of creation (Khayr al-Barīyah)." (101)

Similar to this Ayah, it is stated elsewhere in the Quran: «انّ شرّ الدّوابّ عند اللّه الصُمّ البُكم الّذین لایعقلون» (102)

The reason for a human being the worst is that the Messenger and the Book came to him, and he understands the truth, but due to arrogance and pride, he insists (on denial). «كفروا من اهل الكتاب... هم شرّ البریّة»

101) Shawāhid al-Tanzīl, Vol. 2, p. 357, quoted from Tafsir Namūneh. 102) Al-Anfal, 22.

Messages

1- Whoever does not accept Islam, whether he is a polytheist (Mushrik) or People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitāb), is a disbeliever (Kāfir). «انّ الّذین كفروا من اهل الكتاب و المشركین»

2- The destiny of the People of the Book and the polytheists is the same. «اهل الكتاب و المشركین فى نار جهنم»

3- The crown jewel of existence, meaning humanity, becomes the worst of creatures due to rejecting logic and clear evidence (Bayyinah). «شرّ البریّة»

4- Man determines his own status through his choice: either the worst of creation (Shar al-Barīyah) and destined for Hell, or the best of creation (Khayr al-Barīyah) and destined for Paradise. «كفروا... شرّ البریّة... آمنوا... خیر البریّة»

5- Comparing disbelief (Kufr) and faith (Īmān) and their ultimate outcomes is the best form of clarification. «شرّ البریّة - خیر البریّة»

6- The growth or decline of other beings is limited, but the growth and decline of man are infinite. «شرّ البریّة - خیر البریّة» (Through faith and righteous deeds, man can even surpass the angels.)

7- Becoming the best depends on faith, and the worst are the people of disbelief. (The word hum (هُم) implies exclusivity/restriction.) «اولئك هم شرّ البریّة... هم خیر البریّة»

8- Faith is fruitful only on the condition that it is accompanied by righteous deeds. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات» (But disbelief alone is sufficient cause for Hellfire.)

Ayah

جَزَآؤُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ رَّضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِىَ رَبَّهُۥ (۸)
Their reward, near their Lord, is the Gardens of Eden, with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is for those who fear their Lord. (8)

Key Points

Khashyah (خشیت) refers to the fear that is based on reverence and awe. This verse states that Paradise is exclusively for the people of khashyah: «ذلك لمن خشى ربّه» (That is for him who fears his Lord). And another verse states that only the scholars (ulama) possess khashyah: «انّما یخشى اللّه من عباده العلماء»(103) (Only those of His servants who have knowledge fear Allah). Therefore, Paradise is exclusive to the scholars. On the other hand, we know that not all scholars and learned people are inhabitants of Paradise. This is because the Quran considers many of the inhabitants of Hellfire to be those who went astray after having knowledge and awareness: «اضلّه اللّه على علم»(104) (Allah led him astray knowingly). Similarly, not all illiterate people are inhabitants of Hellfire. Therefore, the knowledge that causes khashyah is not this conventional knowledge, but rather a natural and divine understanding that leads to the illumination of the heart.

103) Fatir, 28. 104) Jathiyah, 23.

Messages

1- The Paradise of the body consists of gardens and rivers, and the Paradise of the soul is the pleasure (satisfaction) of Allah. «جنّات عدن... رضى اللّه عنهم»

2- Divine khashyah is the source of acting upon divine commands. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات... جزاؤهم... جنّات... ذلك لمن خشى ربّه»

3- The eternity of Paradise for the believers is certain. (The words ‘Adn (عدن), khalidīn (خالدین), and abadan (ابداً) all indicate the certainty of eternal dwelling.) «جنّات عدن... خالدین فیها ابداً»
